verses.1: “Isn’t a man forced to labor on earth?
verses.2: As a servant who earnestly desires the shadow,
verses.3: so I am made to possess months of misery,
verses.4: When I lie down, I say,
verses.5: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust.
verses.6: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle,
verses.7: Oh remember that my life is a breath.
verses.8: The eye of him who sees me will see me no more.
verses.9: As the cloud is consumed and vanishes away,
verses.10: He will return no more to his house,
verses.12: Am I a sea, or a sea monster,
verses.13: When I say, ‘My bed will comfort me.
verses.14: then you scare me with dreams
verses.15: so that my soul chooses strangling,
verses.17: What is man, that you should magnify him,
verses.18: that you should visit him every morning,
verses.19: How long will you not look away from me,
verses.20: If I have sinned, what do I do to you, you watcher of men?
verses.21: Why do you not pardon my disobedience, and take away my iniquity?
